issues.description: The '3.8 million new cells per second' figure is widely cited (Sender & Milo 2021) but is a whole-body estimate that includes red blood cell and gut epithelial turnover, not a count of mitotic decisions happening at a checkpoint right now. Phrasing 'each one of them just made a decision — divide, or don't' conflates differentiated turnover with G1 restriction-point commitment. Suggest softening to 'your body produces millions of new cells every second, and each one had to pass a checkpoint to get there.'

Statement 'in G1 ... the DNA is still one copy per chromosome' is imprecise and AP-exam-risky. In G1 each chromosome consists of a single chromatid (one DNA molecule), but the ploidy is still 2n in a somatic cell — students confuse 'one copy per chromosome' with 'haploid.' Also: centrosome duplication actually begins at the G1/S transition and continues through S, not exclusively in G2 as implied. Fix: 'In G1 each chromosome is a single chromatid; centrosomes begin duplicating around G1/S; by G2 the cell has finished duplicating its centrosomes and is assembling the components it needs for the spindle.'

Claim that 'PMAT ... is the framing the AP College Board uses too' is misleading — the current CED lists five phases including prometaphase (see CED Unit 4). The script then teaches five anyway, so the framing contradicts itself. Drop the PMAT-is-College-Board line.

'Separase cleaves cohesin' is correct but the script omits that the anaphase-promoting complex/cyclosome (APC/C) is what triggers separase activation by degrading securin. Not required by CED but a frequent FRQ-level distinction; mention APC/C in one clause for completeness.

'CDK concentrations stay roughly constant' is acceptable shorthand but glosses over the fact that CDK *activity* is also gated by inhibitory phosphorylation (Wee1) and activating phosphorylation (CAK), not just cyclin binding. For AP-level this is fine; for a student going into AP FRQ depth, one sentence on phosphorylation as a second layer of control would tighten this.

Saying tumor suppressors are 'recessive at the cell level' is the classic Knudson two-hit framing and is correct for Rb and to a large extent p53, but p53 also exhibits well-documented dominant-negative behavior where one mutant allele can poison the tetramer. The script's blanket 'both alleles need to be knocked out' is the AP-expected answer, so this is a minor nuance rather than a fix-required error.

summary: Script is solid AP-level cell-cycle content and tracks the CED closely. One critical clarity fix in section 05 (chromatid vs. chromosome copy / centrosome timing), plus a handful of minor tightenings around APC/C, CDK regulation, and the PMAT-vs-CED framing.
